name: Change Kernel
summary: Owns the trusted Change state machine and produces content-addressed Accepted Change Packages.

invariants: Workers and submitted Evidence cannot ratify their own output.

Builtin, direct Gate, and authority-approved cross-Claim Evidence cover Claims only through their frozen compiler-owned Verification Obligation mapping; Gate Evidence issuance and consumption both require the exact eligible Gate and command route, and cross-Claim coverage additionally binds the exact target and an envelope source Claim.
Acceptance binds exact governance, repository content, Evidence, Closure, and authority.
Outcome alignment inputs and compiler-produced alignment are bound into the Verification Subject and Accepted Change Package.
Outcome exceptions require the frozen Development Plan authority and can never grant progress or transition proof.
Acceptance re-derives compiler-owned Change projections from the frozen Governance Baseline before sealing them.
Every Candidate freezes a content-addressed catalog of prior Accepted Packages; plan-amendment Transition proof is re-resolved before Gate execution and acceptance.
Within the local single-writer Change Store, a later historically Accepted Project Model amendment makes every earlier Candidate stale before compilation, Gate execution, or acceptance.
Declared Knowledge Gap Closure Contracts are rechecked before ordinary Change compilation, Gate execution, and acceptance; only the Outcome Transition compiler may authorize a Gap closure delta.
A Change id can produce at most one Accepted Package; invalidated historical acceptance requires a new follow-up Change.
Plan amendments cannot remove or reinterpret stable Outcome Criteria or add Criteria during an Outcome status transition.

Project Model owns strict planRefs value semantics; Change Kernel owns presence-based planRef alias conflicts and must adapt create and patch inputs before persistence so the Store contains only canonical planRefs.

Project and Change read queries fail closed unless at most three observations contain two consecutive identical source digests; a stabilized Change query observes Model, Git, and Store once per round, projects every record without writes, preserves historical state and acceptance, and returns bounded list summaries while full Evidence and package bodies remain detail-only.

Each Change write lifecycle operation compiles one Project Model-owned opaque path ownership product from one stabilized exact Model and tracked-path observation; Context Capsule generation and touched or prospective path enforcement consume that same process-local product, frozen/current ownership drift fails closed, and Kernel never interprets ownership selectors or dispositions.

Context Session preparation is a read-pure Change Kernel query over one stabilized composite physical repository identity, Model, Git, and Store snapshot. Each observation brackets Model and Git reads with the Assurance Runtime repository identity seam, and only two adjacent rounds whose complete composite digests and physical identities agree may issue. Preparation selects one current unaccepted compiled Change, rederives its compiler-owned projection and current scope only from that snapshot and the frozen Governance Baseline, requires its frozen Model and Git bindings to equal the stabilized sources, derives the exact Project Model materialization plan, compilation binding, canonical Worker Work Specification, logical Capsule and closed self-digested create document itself, and returns that ordinary document beside one frozen zero-own-key process-local Context Session Create Product. The factory-bound product projector first authenticates the matching exact create digest and private repository-path expectation, then rechecks one stabilized composite snapshot against every issuer-bound repository identity, Model, Git, tracked-path, and Store digest before returning only a defensive body-free projection that omits the private path; it rejects forged, serialized, cloned, proxied, cross-instance, cross-repository, stale, or mismatched products. A successful projection is point-in-time launch authority for only that exact snapshot-bound attempt, not a lease or acceptance: mutation before projection fails stale, mutation after projection does not retroactively rewrite an in-flight attempt, the product remains reusable for downstream failure retry, and Context Kernel owns expected-empty lineage commit and successful-replay exclusion. Preparation and projection never mutate the Change Store, create Evidence, accept a Change, or authorize Context expansion.

The fixed brownfield relay Change is an ordinary Kernel lifecycle subject: no adoption mode may bypass stabilized ownership, Outcome contribution, exact Contract-visible Claim routing, Gate Evidence, open Knowledge Gap projection, Knowledge Closure, authority, scope analysis, or content-addressed acceptance, and its initial compiled Context may describe dependency Contracts without claiming Worker read enforcement.

An Architecture Profile query compiles one Project Model-owned Claim Gate route product covering every Contract Claim in the stabilized current Model, passes that exact process-local product into Profile compilation, and compiles at most one product per unique verified frozen Governance Baseline over the union of actually referenced non-builtin source Claims; one query-level ceiling charges each unique product's compiler-owned workUnits, routes, and totalRouteBytes plus bounded Kernel route projection work once, cache hits are not recharged, and operational observations never become assurance or Evidence.

An Architecture Profile window query selects one canonical bounded Change-id window from one stabilized composite snapshot before normalizing Change facts or compiling frozen-baseline route products; each nonterminal result carries one process-authenticated opaque continuation bound to the exact snapshot, query shape, ordering policy, and last emitted Change, so unchanged-snapshot traversal is complete and duplicate-free while malformed, modified, expired, unissued, cross-process, cross-snapshot, or cross-query continuations fail closed without work outside the selected window.

One stabilized Workbench query reuses the Architecture Profile's current-Model route product and consumes Project Model's bounded module Claim/Gate projection over each corresponding current or frozen-Plan route product for all authoring visibility and acceptance routes; it does not traverse Contract publicContracts or dependency via relations, interpret Gate or command scope, or filter route effectiveModuleRefs. Change Kernel alone projects lifecycle-permitted compile, Gate, and accept actions, validates exact Claim-route annotations from the compiler-owned Verification Plan, preserves otherwise runnable Gates that cover no Change Claim, and emits every unavailable action's non-empty duplicate-free disabled-reason code subset in declared precedence order; no adapter may reconstruct these decisions from raw Project Model or Change fields.

The stabilized Workbench projection terminates Claim eligibility in Kernel through a bounded canonical claimSelectionRoutes collection keyed by exact Change kind, selected Outcome or null, and primary Module; every route covers each visible Claim exactly once, consumes compiler-projected integrity protection before emission, and carries the final selectable boolean plus ordered closed reasons, so adapters perform only exact route lookup and never receive protected-Claim sets as policy input.

The same stabilized Workbench query projects exact authoring Plan-selection and selected-Change acceptance-input requirements through the compiler and lifecycle validator policy seams that enforce them; the bounded source-bound product contains closed Change-kind, cardinality, option, reason, field, Closure, authority, Decision, amendment, adoption, and confirmation semantics, so adapters only select and submit declared values.
Integration requires current full assurance when policy demands it.
